memoryArtifacts.description: Origin Displayed: English - Spelling variations of this family name include: Folkard, Fokard, Folkart, Folkert, Foucard and many more. First found in Devonshire, where they settled after 1066 C.E. Radulfus Fochart was registered in Normandy in 1198. Some of the first settlers of this family name or some of its variants were: Peter Folkert, who sailed to Maryland in 1825; Martin Folkert, who sailed to Maryland in 1819 and one Mr. Fouchard sailed to San Francisco in 1851. (From www.HouseOfNames.com Archives copyright  2000 - 2009).  Derived from the German and Dutch Folker or Folckers, the name means 'The descendent of Foleger' an early 8th Century personal name translating as 'People-Spear'. The name is recorded heraldically in Rietstap's Armorial General of The Nederlands, the Coat of Arms being a silver field charged with two serpents in green - the serpent being the ancient sign for wisdom. The plural of the name means 'The Son of Folcher' The first recorded spelling of the family name is shown to be that of Johannes Folchers which was dated C. 1680, in the Provincial State of Holland during the reign of William 3rd of Orange (The Stadtholder) 1650 - 1704 Surnames became necessary when governments introduced personal taxation. In England this was known as Poll Tax. Throughout the centuries, surnames in every country have continued to "develop" often leading to astonishing variants of the original spelling.   Copyright: Name Origin Research www.surnamedb.com 1980 - 2015.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Alice Folcard於大約1445年出生在英國素福爾可希加姆時，她的父親William Folcard18歲，母親Joan Dodington16歲。她於1460年之前與Thomas Coke在英國英格蘭諾福爾可結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1500年在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾格亞特 多丁頓享年56歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭素福爾可。

nameMeanings.name: Folcard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: French: relationship name French Foucquart, from the Old French personal name Foucard (Continental Germanic Fulchard). Compare Folkard .

variants.name: Folkard
meanings: 1 Norman, English: relationship name from the Middle English (Old French) personal name Folcard, Foucard, Focard (Continental Germanic Fulchard). Compare Fulcher , with which this name may sometimes have been confused. See also Flucker .
2 Norman, English: relationship name perhaps also partly from the Continental Germanic personal name Fulchered, which had some currency in E Anglia in the medieval period.

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Alice Folcard was born about 1445, in Higham, Suffolk, England, her father, William Folcard, was 18 and her mother, Joan Dodington, was 16. She married Thomas Coke before 1460, in Norfolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 3 daughters. She died in 1500, in Great Doddington, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 56, and was buried in Suffolk, England, United Kingdom.

Elle a épousé Thomas Coke avant 1460, à Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée en 1500, à Great Doddington, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 56 ans, et a été enterrée à Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Alice Folcardが約1445年にイギリススフォルクヒガムで生まれた時，父William Folcardは18歳，母Joan Dodingtonは16歳であった。1460年以前にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクでThomas Cokeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも8人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1500年にイギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアグレアト ドッドイントンで56歳で死去し，イギリスイギリススフォルクにて埋葬された。

ko.King's Lynn St John, Norfolk, England, U: 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 킨그'스 린 스트 존
ko.lifeSummary: Alice Folcard(이)가 대략 1445년 영국 수폴크 히그함에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Folcard(은)는 18세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan Dodington(은)는 16세였다. 그녀는 1460년 이전 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크에서 Thomas Coke(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1500년 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 그리트 도딩턴에서 56세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크에 묻혔다.

pt.Of, Southmolton, Devonshire, England: South Molton, Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Alice Folcard nasceu aproximadamente 1445, em Higham, Suffolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Folcard, tinha 18 anos e sua mãe, Joan Dodington, tinha 16 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas Coke antes 1460, em Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1500, em Great Doddington, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 56 anos, e foi sepultada em Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.King's Lynn St John, Norfolk, England, U: кинг'с линн ст джон, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Alice Folcard родилась около 1445 г. в местности хаиам, суффолк, Англия, её отцу по имени William Folcard было 18 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Dodington, 16. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Coke перед 1460 г. в местности норфолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 3 дочери. Она умерла в 1500 г. в местности грит доддингтон, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 56 лет и была похоронена в местности суффолк, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Alice Folcard nació aproximadamente 1445, en Higham, Suffolk, Inglaterra, su papá, William Folcard, tenía 18 años y su mamá, Joan Dodington, tenía 16 años. Se casó con Thomas Coke antes de 1460, en Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 8 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió en 1500, en Great Doddington, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 56 años, y fue sepultada en Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
